Module com.bjdelacruz.tictactoe
Package com.bjdelacruz.tictactoe

Class BoardManager

java.lang.Object
com.bjdelacruz.tictactoe.BoardManager
public final class BoardManager extends Object
This class is responsible for creating a new Tic-Tac-Toe game board, creating players for the game, and marking spots on the board for both players. It is also responsible for checking whether a player won or the game ended in a tie.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Player
    createComputerPlayer(BoardManager boardManager)
    Creates a new player that represents the computer and can only be used in a person-vs-computer game.
    static Player
    createHumanPlayer(BoardManager boardManager)
    Creates a new player that represents a human being and can be used in a person-vs-computer or person-vs-person game.
    static BoardManager
    createNewClassicGame()
    Creates a new Tic-Tac-Toe game board with size 3x3.
    String
    getBoard()
    Returns a square grid showing the current state of the game.
    String
    getCurrentPlayerName()
    Returns the name of the player whose turn it is to mark a spot on the game board.
    Optional<Player>
    getWinner()
    Returns the player who won the game.
    boolean
    isGameOver()
    Returns true if a player won or the game ended in a tie.
    boolean
    isTie()
    Returns true if no players can make any more moves and there is no winner.
    boolean
    movePlayerTo(int row, int column)
    Marks a spot on the game board at the given row and column.
    void
    setPlayers(Player player1, Player player2)
    Sets the list of players for this game.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • createNewClassicGame

      public static BoardManager createNewClassicGame()
      Creates a new Tic-Tac-Toe game board with size 3x3.
      Returns:
      A new Tic-Tac-Toe game board.

    • createComputerPlayer

      public static Player createComputerPlayer(BoardManager boardManager)
      Creates a new player that represents the computer and can only be used in a person-vs-computer game.
      Parameters:
      boardManager - The board manager.
      Returns:
      An instance of a Player.

    • createHumanPlayer

      public static Player createHumanPlayer(BoardManager boardManager)
      Creates a new player that represents a human being and can be used in a person-vs-computer or person-vs-person game.
      Parameters:
      boardManager - The board manager.
      Returns:
      An instance of a Player.

    • isTie

      public boolean isTie()
      Returns true if no players can make any more moves and there is no winner.
      Returns:
      True if the game ends in a tie, false otherwise.

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()
      Returns true if a player won or the game ended in a tie.
      Returns:
      True if a player won or there are no more moves to make, false otherwise.

    • getWinner

      public Optional<Player> getWinner()
      Returns the player who won the game. An empty Optional is returned if no one won yet or there is a tie.
      Returns:
      The player who makes the winning move.

    • setPlayers

      public void setPlayers(Player player1, Player player2)
      Sets the list of players for this game.
      Parameters:
      player1 - The first player.
      player2 - The second player.

    • movePlayerTo

      public boolean movePlayerTo(int row, int column)
      Marks a spot on the game board at the given row and column.
      Parameters:
      row - The row in the board, 1 <= row <= size.
      column - The row in the board, 1 <= column <= size.
      Returns:
      True if a spot was successfully marked on the board, false otherwise (i.e. the player did not make a move).

    • getCurrentPlayerName

      public String getCurrentPlayerName()
      Returns the name of the player whose turn it is to mark a spot on the game board.
      Returns:
      The name of the current player.

    • getBoard

      public String getBoard()
      Returns a square grid showing the current state of the game.
      Returns:
      A square grid that shows the current state of the game.
